msgs.text:
I read the prompt and the 55-turn proposal log as external work, not as proof. The prompt fixes a very specific submission format: generators sigma,tau,..., with sigma,tau distinguished and the tame relation implicit, and all profinite exponents represented modulo M=85667662080.The only candidate that survives the prior finite-quotient attacks is still the dynamic tame-inertia word:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2∈Z and η=2π on odd tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,where π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η≡50932630783(modM).The prior log’s latest concrete computation is Turn 55: in the mixed quotient (e,f)=(5,4), after correcting the H-equivariant lift of the invariant b-class and replacing the naive square-only affine origin by the actual quadratic originΘbact=h∈H∑Xh[2]+h∈H∑[Xh,Xhσ],the candidate’s first marked IA residual satisfiesmbcorr−Θbact=0.That is a real improvement over merely saying “no counterexample was found.” My new contribution is to explain why this Turn 55 vanishing should not be treated as an isolated (5,4) miracle. It is a formal consequence of using the actual quadratic relation of the candidate as the affine origin.

Let Δ=Δe,f, write f=2a m with m odd, let

H=⟨τˉ,σˉ2a⟩,P=Δ/H≃C2a,and let S=σf be the marked pro-2 unramified element. In the reduced degree-one model,L1≃F2 [Δ]⊕F2 b⊕F2S.

A marked lift α of the P-generator satisfiesα2a=Inn(S).Ifmb=α(b)b−1∈L2H,then the marked power condition gives the affine norm equationNP(mb)=[S,b].

Turn 53 correctly observed that H1(P,L2H) classifies differences of marked lifts, not the raw mb of one lift. Turn 54 then introduced a square-only particular solution Θb, and Turn 55 corrected it to the particular solution coming from the actual quadratic initial relation qact of the candidate.Here is the general mechanism. After making the H-equivariant correction to b, the candidate’s Schreier relations around the P-cycle have the schematic degree-two formα(bp)=bp+1 ⋅exp(Θp)+O(L3),where p∈P, b0=b, and Θp∈L2H is the p-slice of the X-only quadratic part of the actual relation. Multiplying these equations around the full P-cycle gives p∈P∑Θp=[S,b].But that same equality is exactly the X-part of the candidate’s actual quadratic Demuškin relationqact=Qact+[b,S],Qact=p∈P∑Θp.

Thus the natural affine origin is not arbitrary:Θbact=Θ0.

For the candidate itself,mbcorr=Θ0,sombcorr−Θbact=0 at the first marked IA layer for every finite tame quotient, provided the same H-equivariant b-lift and the actual candidate quadratic relation are used.This reframes the next stage. The seven-dimensional first IA ambiguity in (e,f)=(5,4), and the larger involution-indexed spaces in cases such as (17,8) or (91,12), are still real difference spaces. But candidate-side residuals should vanish after the correct affine normalization. Therefore repeating Turn 55 as a candidate-side computation in (17,8) or (91,12) is unlikely to reveal a new obstruction; it will mostly reverify the same telescoping identity.The remaining hard problem is now more sharply arithmetic: prove that the arithmetic local Galois formation uses the same affine origin qact, or equivalently that the arithmetic-minus-candidate difference class vanishes inH1(P,L2H)and then in the higher filtered analogues.The two algebraic conventions from the prior log that should remain frozen are:(1−tαa)Da=1−t,Da=(1−t)(1−tαa)−1,for the sign-cover norm coefficient on nontrivial tame-inertia character blocks, and (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T−s for the trivial tame-inertia elimination. These avoid the reciprocal errors that appeared repeatedly in the earlier turns.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I generalized Turn 55 conceptually: the vanishingmbcorr−Θbact=0 is not special to (e,f)=(5,4); it follows formally at the first marked IA layer whenever the affine origin is defined from the candidate’s actual quadratic relation qact. Thus H1(P,L2H) should be used only for arithmetic-minus-candidate differences, not for raw candidate coordinates.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is an arithmetic comparison or dyadic formation theorem showing that the actual local Galois action has the same filtered affine origins and hence zero arithmetic-minus-candidate class over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: Stop recomputing candidate residuals alone. For (e,f)=(5,4), fix a concrete tame degree-20 extension and Frobenius-lift convention, compute the arithmetic first IA class using the same H-equivariant b-lift and the same qact -normalization, and test whether the arithmetic-minus-candidate class vanishes.[BREAKTHROUGH]
